calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2024 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2023 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-23-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default Against Defendant Cross-Defendant Gabriel De Jesus Arias
Off Calendar Sep-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

*Court acknowledges that plaintiff's opposition was filed 11 days late.*

DEFENDANT GABRIEL ARIAS' Motion To Set Aside Default Against Defendant Cross-Defendant Gabriel De Jesus Arias

Gabriel de Jesus Arias' unopposed motion to set aside default is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Continued To Sep-27-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default Against Defendant Cross-Defendant Gabriel De Jesus Arias
Off Calendar Aug-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Isabella Stepanyan, Esq., DRAKE LAW FIRM, 888-315-5721, Izabella@drakelawgroup.com
for Plaintiff EDGAR DANIEL REYES COUTINO.
Carmen Gutman, Esq., CHOLAKIAN & ASSOCIATES, 650-871-9544, cgutman@cholakian.net for Defendant GABRIEL DE JESUS ARIAS
Raquel Sharp, CSR#10619, 925-226-8222, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, Court Reporter

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as modified.

DEFENDANT GABRIEL ARIAS' Motion To Set Aside Default Against Defendant Cross-Defendant Gabriel De Jesus Arias.

Defendant and cross-defendant's motion to set aside default is denied without prejudice.

Because no attorney affidavit of fault was provided, relief is discretionary and must be based on a showing of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (CCP 473(b).) The party moving for relief on the basis of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect" must show specific facts demonstrating that one of these conditions was met. (Hopkins & Carley v. Gens (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1401, 1410, 135 [defendant's burden to demonstrate "that due to some mistake, either of fact or of law, of himself or of his counsel, or through some inadvertence, surprise or neglect which may properly be considered excusable, the judgment or order from which he seeks relief should be reversed."].)

Here, defendant does not on state which ground he seeks relief or why the failure should be excused. Additionally, no declaration or other evidence was provided to show "mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect." Plaintiff must specify the actual cause of his failure to present the defense the first time around, and explain why that failure should be excused. (Hopkins, 200 Cal.App.4th at p. 1410.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312
